qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated login endpoint denial-of-service via bcrypt computational exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different auth code path and root cause: this finding targets POST /api/login bcrypt work without throttling, while the advisory targets public signup applying default admin permissions to new users.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Shell command injection via WebSocket allowlist bypass in command runner
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different component and root cause: this finding targets /api/command WebSocket command allowlist bypass under shell execution configuration, not self-registration inheriting Perm.Admin from user defaults.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Authenticated unbounded file upload denial-of-service via disk exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different endpoint and sink: this finding targets POST /api/resources writeFile/io.Copy upload size limits, while the advisory targets /api/signup and UserDefaults.Apply copying admin permission.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause: signupHandler creating a new user, applying settings.Defaults via UserDefaults.Apply, and failing to clear Perm.Admin for self-registered accounts.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-5gg9-5g7w-hm73 describes unauthenticated POST /api/signup granting administrator privileges when signup is enabled and defaults.perm.admin is true.
login-bcrypt-dos targets POST /api/login bcrypt CPU exhaustion and missing throttling, not signup or default permission application.
shell-command-injection-via-websocket targets /api/command WebSocket command parsing and shell allowlist bypass, not user creation or admin permission defaults.
unbounded-upload-dos targets POST /api/resources and writeFile/io.Copy without upload size limits, not self-registration authorization.
